tools.name: scrape_instagram
tools.description: Scrape any public Instagram URL (post, reel, profile, story, highlight) via the Apify `apify/instagram-scraper` actor. Returns the caption, media URLs, like/comment counts, hashtags, mentions, owner, and timestamp — content WebFetch/oEmbed can't see (Meta gates them). OPTIONAL-UNTIL-CREDENTIALED: when the user has not stored an Apify API token in admin, this tool does NOT call Apify; it returns `ok:false code:no_token` with guidance to add the token. Never calls Apify without the user's token.

url.description: An instagram.com URL (post /p/, reel /reel/, profile, story).

mode.description: Output shape. `json` = full dataset item (default); `caption` = caption text only; `summary` = human-readable digest with engagement counts.

tools.name: scrape_x
tools.description: Scrape any public X (Twitter) URL — tweet, thread, profile, or long-form Article — via Apify (kaitoeasyapi tweet scraper; fastcrawler article-to-markdown for `--article`). Returns the real content, not the React shell WebFetch sees. Accepts x.com, twitter.com, and the bare /i/status/<id> form. OPTIONAL-UNTIL-CREDENTIALED: when the user has not stored an Apify API token in admin, this tool does NOT call Apify; it returns `ok:false code:no_token` with guidance to add the token. Never calls Apify without the user's token.

url.description: An x.com / twitter.com URL (tweet, profile, or /i/status/<id>).

mode.description: Output shape. `json` = full dataset items (default); `text` = handle + timestamp + body; `summary` = digest with engagement counts; `article` = X long-form Article body as markdown (URL must be the share-tweet URL, not /i/article/<id>).

thread.description: When true, fetch the full conversation and return only the original author's tweets (keeps the thread coherent). Ignored in `article` mode.

secrets.name: apify_api_token
secrets.label: apify
secrets.install_prompt: Paste your Apify API token (https://apify.com — free tier ~2000 results/month, no card). Optional: without it, Instagram/X scraping stays hidden and no-ops. Find it under Apify Console → Settings → Integrations → API token.
